channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Inauguration Day is tomorrow – Here’s a complete guide to Fox News Channel’s coverage and what to expect
item.description: Fox News Channel and Fox News Digital is your home to wall-to-wall coverage of President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's departing UN envoy says US rivals will fill the vacuum if Trump abandons global leadership
item.description: The outgoing U.S. ambassador to the United Nations says she watched America’s leadership diminish in the world during Donald Trump’s first presidency and saw China fill the vacuum.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thousands march in Washington days before Trump takes office
item.description: The march is just one of several protests, rallies and vigils focused on abortion, rights, immigration rights and the Israel-Hamas war planned in advance of inauguration Monday. Around the country, over 350 similar marches are taking place in every state.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, ASHRAF KHALIL and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of left-wing demonstrators descend on Washington to protest Trump inauguration
item.description: Thousands protesters advocating for a range of left wing causes have descended on Washington, D.C., to protest President–elect Trump’s inauguration on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NY Times reporter roasted after 'unitary executive theory' flub in Trump OMB nominee story
item.description: A New York Times reporter stepped into controversy this week when critics say he fundamentally misunderstood a constitutional law doctrine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump spent Saturday evening in Washington celebrating with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted will succeed JD Vance in the US Senate
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Republican Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted will succeed Vice President-elect JD Vance in the U.S. Senate, the state's governor announced Friday. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court will hear case of Maryland parents who object to LGBTQ books in their kids' classes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday agreed to hear an appeal from parents in suburban Washington who want to pull their children from elementary school classes that use books featuring LGBTQ characters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Senate advances a migrant detention bill that could be Trump's first law to sign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate is heading toward a final vote on legislation that would give states the power to challenge federal immigration policies and require federal authorities to detain migrants accused of theft and violent crimes, setting a new tone on immigration as Donald Trump enters the W
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley Act, teeing up final vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday to advance the Laken Riley Act, putting Republicans a step closer to sending the first bill of the 119th Congress to President-elect Trump for his signature next week and giving him an early win on one of his key issues.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court upholds law banning TikTok if it’s not sold by its Chinese parent company
item.description: The Supreme Court has unanimously upheld the federal law banning TikTok beginning Sunday unless it’s sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What’s next for Kamala Harris
item.description: Inauguration Day marks the first time in more than 20 years that Kamala Harris will not be in public office. “It is not my nature to go quietly into the night,” she told allies on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: The photos were revealed on Trump's website in a post titled "Official Portraits Released — And They Go Hard ????" and show Trump with his signature near-scowl and Vance with arms crossed, smiling.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: No taxes on tips comes closer to reality with Cortez Masto, Rosen push
item.description: No taxes on tips is becoming a closer reality for millions of workers after Democratic Nevada Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen and other senators reintroduced the proposal Thursday, her office said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate adds amendment to immigration bill named after Laken Riley
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday adopted the first amendment to the Laken Riley Act, as Republicans push for a legislative win to open the new Congress. The legislation in its current form would mandate federal detention of immigrants without legal status accused of theft, burglary and other related crimes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Fetterman's approach to Trump stirs speculation
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman’s (D-Pa.) open-minded approach to President-elect Trump is fueling questions about his motives and vexing some of his fellow Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Rudy Giuliani’s Florida condo, World Series rings on the line in latest trial
item.description: Rudy Giuliani will again square off in court against two Georgia election workers Thursday, this time seeking to keep his Florida condominium and other prized possessions from the women who won a $146 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota courts may get final say in government's constitutional clash
item.description: An ugly opening to Minnesota’s legislative session and a campaign season that won’t end is fueling lawsuit after lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Walz unveils slimmer Minnesota budget, pitches sales tax cut
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz on Thursday pitched a tighter two-year budget, complete with spending pullbacks and other measures to keep a potential deficit at bay. He also proposed a lower state sales tax rate but called for extending the tax’s reach to financial services.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: TikTok ban to impact local businesses, content creators
item.description: San Diego is home to talented and creative content creators who make a living on TikTok. While many are able to make a full-time income on the app, some are also helping small businesses in return, who say the app has inadvertently helped them tremendously.
item.dc:creator: Zara Barker, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay native Pam Bondi goes through Senate confirmation hearings to become U.S. Attorney General
item.description: Pam Bondi grew up in Temple Terrace, she worked in the Hillsborough County State Attorney's office as a prosecutor, and she served two terms as the elected Florida State Attorney General.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court weighs postponing election crucial to Capitol control
item.description: Minnesota Supreme Court justices on Wednesday promised to decide quickly whether a special Roseville-area election critical to deciding power in the House happens as scheduled on Jan. 28 or gets pushed deeper into the legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Murphy calling for cellphone ban in NJ classrooms
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (PIX11) - Could there be a day when there will be no cellphones in New Jersey classrooms? In his State of the State address on Tuesday, Gov. Phil Murphy announced his support for legislation that would direct school districts to adopt policies that ban cellphones in K-12 classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: “Kids can get online porn through gaming systems, tablets, phones, computers. Let me just say that content filtering for all those different devices, I can say from personal experience, is difficult to keep up with,” said Justice Amy Coney Barrett, who has seven children.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: After half a century in politics, Biden bids farewell in an Oval Office address
item.description: President Biden delivered a farewell speech to the nation from the Oval Office tonight — a chance for him to frame his own legacy one last time before he leaves the White House.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: NYC mayor: More beds for mentally ill people, more cops on subways
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- With more NYPD officers heading into the subway system during the overnight hours, New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he is also looking to get mentally ill homeless people off the trains with a new option.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Pam Bondi grilled over Trump's influence at AG hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Pam Bondi, President-elect Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Justice, echoed attacks on the agency she would lead if confirmed, but demurred on how she might handle tests of its independence. Bondi is a more traditional pick to lead the department than Trump’s initial choice, former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: End to daylight saving time among bills for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: A Republican lawmaker who represents parts of six rural Nevada counties is sponsoring a bill to take the state off daylight saving time — one of the 200 bills written so far.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis hopes incoming Trump administration will kickstart crackdown on illegal immigration
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis will be speaking in Winter Haven on Wednesday morning at the Polk County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida's Rubio and Bondi face confirmation hearings
item.description: Former state attorney general and Tampa native Pam Bondi and Senator Marco Rubio are set to appear on Capitol Hill for confirmation hearings, Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Few US adults confident Justice Department and FBI will act fairly under Trump, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: As President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, prepares for questioning from senators on Wednesday, only about 2 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Departing DHS Secretary Mayorkas contends he delivered border security in the end
item.description: As DHS Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas prepares to leave office, NPR sits down for an exit interview. He tells us the border is more secure now than before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Milton Guevara and Steve Inskeep, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota legislative standoff spills out of the Statehouse into the courthouse
item.description: With accusations flying over which party is ignoring state law, the fight to control the Minnesota House is shifting from lawmakers to lawyers. DFL Secretary of State Steve Simon is calling on the Minnesota Supreme Court to weigh in on who has what power.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump will begin his presidency in delicate position, poll finds
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll shows that Americans' support for President-elect Donald Trump's top priorities is split, despite his claims of a mandate for his agenda.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News
